name: Backtrader (Python Backtesting Library)

auth.notes: Local library; broker auth for live trading

performance.notes: Pure Python; large datasets can be slow without optimization

agent_readiness.notes: Event-driven backtesting framework for trading strategies. class MyStrategy(bt.Strategy): def next(self). Cerebro engine: cerebro.addstrategy(MyStrategy). Data feeds (pandas, CSV, yfinance, IB, Interactive Brokers). Analyzers (returns, drawdown, Sharpe). Indicators (SMA, RSI, MACD, etc.). Order types (market, limit, stop). Portfolio management. Multi-asset. Live trading via Interactive Brokers. Matplotlib plotting. Still widely used despite limited maintenance.

gotchas: Limited recent maintenance — some Python 3.10+ compatibility issues
Slow for large datasets vs vectorbt (vectorized)
Complex architecture with steep learning curve
Matplotlib dependency for plots can be tricky on headless servers
best_when: Event-driven strategy backtesting with order simulation
Multi-asset portfolio backtesting with position sizing
Strategy development with Interactive Brokers live trading
Learning algorithmic trading with community examples
avoid_when: High-frequency or large dataset backtesting (vectorbt faster)
Modern Python projects (vectorbt or zipline-reloaded more maintained)
Pure analysis without live trading needs
summary: Backtrader is the most-used Python event-driven backtesting library. class Strategy(bt.Strategy): def next(). Cerebro engine orchestrates data + strategy + broker. Built-in indicators (SMA, RSI, MACD). Multiple data feeds. Analyzers for performance metrics. Interactive Brokers live trading. Matplotlib charts. Limited recent maintenance but large community and examples. Slower than vectorbt for large datasets.
